description: Pattern catalogue for the scan-edit-write PreToolUse hook. Maps 1:1 to PLAN.md §3 threat-model addendum (M1.6, PR #18). Edit this file to add a pattern; you do not need to touch the hook script.
skips_note: path_substrings is the implementation-level equivalent of the path_globs listed in PLAN.md §3 (**/__fixtures__/**, **/test*/**/*.fixture.*, docs/**). Substring matching avoids a fragile glob-to-regex conversion in bash and catches the same set of files for the patterns in this catalogue.

patterns.name: eval-unsanitised-js
patterns.description: Unsanitised eval() of user input in JS/TS
patterns.rationale: eval() of unsanitised user input is a code-injection vector. Use JSON.parse() for data or a real interpreter for code.

patterns.name: exec-unsanitised-node
patterns.description: Unsanitised exec/execSync/spawnSync of user input in Node
patterns.rationale: Shell-command injection via exec() of user input. Use execFile() with an arg array instead.

patterns.name: hardcoded-secret-generic
patterns.description: Hardcoded secret assignment (api_key/token/password/secret) with high-entropy literal
patterns.rationale: Credential committed to source. Move to env vars or a secret manager.

patterns.name: hardcoded-secret-known-prefix
patterns.description: Known credential prefix (sk-, gho_, github_pat_, AKIA, …)
patterns.rationale: Recognised credential prefix detected. These never belong in source.

patterns.name: sql-injection-shape
patterns.description: SQL template string with embedded user input
patterns.rationale: Possible SQL injection — use parameterised queries / prepared statements.

patterns.name: shell-injection-shape
patterns.description: Shell exec/spawn with template-string-embedded user input
patterns.rationale: Possible shell injection — pass arguments as an array (execFile) instead of building a shell string.

patterns.name: eval-unsanitised-python
patterns.description: Unsanitised eval()/exec() of user input in Python
patterns.rationale: Python eval/exec on user input is a code-injection vector. Use ast.literal_eval() or a real parser.

patterns.name: csp-unsafe-eval-or-frame-allow-all
patterns.description: CSP relaxation: unsafe-eval, or X-Frame-Options allow-all
patterns.rationale: Security header weakened — confirm this is intentional and documented.
